message:
feat(mux/tab): introduce floating-pane primitives and strengthen split constraint enforcement

This commit substantially extends tab pane management and split-safety behavior.

Floating-pane support added:
- Adds floating pane model/state to TabInner:
  - floating_panes collection
  - floating_focus tracking
  - z-order and visibility state
- Introduces public floating-pane APIs on Tab:
  - add_floating_pane
  - set_floating_pane_rect
  - set_floating_pane_visible
  - set_floating_pane_focus
  - bring_floating_pane_to_front
  - remove_floating_pane
  - iter_floating_panes
- Adds floating geometry/value types:
  - FloatingPaneRect
  - PositionedFloatingPane
- Adds rectangle clamping and resize helpers, including minimum floating pane dimensions.
- Updates membership/count semantics so floating panes participate in tab containment and counts.

Resize/split safety hardening:
- Extends split sizing/validation paths to reject invalid requests that cannot satisfy
  pane constraints.
- Adds/expands tests around split-size clamping, constraint compatibility, and resize behavior.

Test coverage included in this file:
- floating-pane add/focus/visibility/remove lifecycle behavior
- top-level split rejection for incompatible constraints
- split resize invariants and fixed-pane resize behavior checks

Why:
- Provides foundational tab-level primitives needed for richer layout control while
  tightening correctness around constraint-driven split operations.
